profile.name: Tom Fletcher
profile.headline: The Remote Worker · books longer stays to live and work
profile.bio: Tom is 51, a freelance product consultant who works from wherever he books. He takes places for two to six weeks at a time and needs them to double as an office: real wifi, a desk, somewhere to take calls. He reads the listing for signals that it fits a working life, not a weekend. Draft persona, sharpen with persona-refine.

facts.label: Cares about
facts.value: Wifi, a desk, quiet for calls

facts.label: Wants
facts.value: A stay that works as an office

sections.title: Jobs to be done
content: Find a longer stay he can actually work from, wifi, desk, quiet, without guessing.
Settle in fast and treat the place as home base for a few weeks.
Extend or adjust the stay without a painful rebooking dance.

sections.title: Pains
content: Listings bury the essentials, he can't tell if the wifi holds a video call until he's there.
Weekend-optimised places don't fit a working week: no desk, thin walls, no long-stay rate.
Rebooking or extending is clunky when a project runs long.

sections.title: Lifestyle
sections.content: Tom's home and office are wherever he last booked. A stay is a few weeks of his real life, so the boring things, bandwidth, a chair that doesn't wreck his back, a quiet corner, matter more than the view.
